user.content: :mention[Dust]{sId=dust} Can you recommend a good Python book? I've been learning the basics and want to go deeper.

agent.content:
I'd recommend **Fluent Python** by Luciano Ramalho. It's an excellent intermediate-to-advanced book that covers Python's data model, iterators, generators, concurrency, and metaprogramming. It's currently in stock on shelf B-12.

Another great option is **Python Cookbook** by David Beazley, available on shelf C-03. It's more recipe-based and great for learning practical patterns.

title: Looking for a book on distributed systems

user.content: :mention[Dust]{sId=dust} Do we have any books on distributed systems? I already read Designing Data-Intensive Applications last year.

agent.content:
Yes! We have **Distributed Systems** by Maarten van Steen in the catalog. It's on shelf A-07 and currently available.

Since you've already read Designing Data-Intensive Applications, you might also enjoy **Site Reliability Engineering** by Betsy Beyer which covers distributed systems from an operational perspective. It's on shelf A-09.
